resources.title: 5G Standalone Testbed
resources.subtitle: Full 5G SA stack with core, RU, IMS, and MEC
resources.description: A complete 5G standalone testbed comprising the 5G core network, radio unit, IMS server, MEC server, and centralised network management system.
resources.overview: The Advanced ECE Lab hosts a full 5G standalone testbed that enables researchers and students to experiment with end-to-end 5G communications. The setup includes the 5G core network (AMF, SMF, UPF), a radio unit, IMS for voice-over-5G services, a multi-access edge computing (MEC) server for latency-sensitive applications, and a centralised network management interface. This testbed supports research in network slicing, QoS, and edge computing.

specs.val: AMF, SMF, UPF, NRF, PCF, AUSF

resources.title: RIS / ORIS Testbed
resources.subtitle: Reconfigurable and Oscillating Intelligent Surface platforms
resources.description: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS) and Oscillating Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (ORIS) testbeds for smart radio environment research.
resources.overview: The AEL's RIS and ORIS testbeds are research-grade platforms for studying smart electromagnetic environments. Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces allow passive beamforming and signal reflection control without active RF chains. The ORIS variant introduces mechanical oscillation to dynamically alter the reflection pattern. These testbeds are used in research on next-generation wireless channel control, energy-efficient communications, and beyond-5G systems.

resources.title: D2D Communication Testbed
resources.subtitle: Device-to-device communication platform
resources.description: A device-to-device (D2D) communication testbed for studying direct wireless links between user devices without traversing the base station.
resources.overview: The D2D testbed in AEL enables research on direct device-to-device wireless communication, bypassing the cellular infrastructure for short-range, low-latency links. This is used for proximity services (ProSe), V2X (vehicle-to-vehicle) emulation, and offloading research. The testbed integrates with the 5G core for hybrid D2D-cellular research scenarios.
applications: V2X emulation and vehicular network research

resources.title: High-Performance Workstations
resources.subtitle: GPU-accelerated systems for signal processing and ML
resources.description: High-performance desktop workstations equipped with dedicated GPUs for real-time signal processing, machine learning, and prototyping.
resources.overview: The Advanced ECE Lab has a set of high-performance workstations used for computationally intensive research tasks including MATLAB simulations, GNU Radio real-time signal processing, deep learning model training, and 5G network simulation. These machines are available to research scholars, interns, and students working on approved projects.
applications: MATLAB and Python signal processing simulations
Deep learning model training for wireless systems

resources.title: Software Defined Radio (SDR) Platforms
resources.subtitle: Flexible RF platforms for waveform prototyping
resources.description: Software defined radio hardware for flexible waveform design, baseband prototyping, and over-the-air wireless experiments.
resources.overview: The AEL maintains a selection of SDR hardware platforms that allow researchers to implement custom waveforms, modulation schemes, and protocols in software. These platforms are used for 5G waveform prototyping, spectrum sensing, cognitive radio experiments, and channel measurement campaigns.
applications: Spectrum sensing and cognitive radio research

resources.title: FPGA Development Boards
resources.subtitle: Xilinx Zynq boards for real-time hardware prototyping
resources.description: Xilinx Zynq FPGA-SoC development boards for real-time hardware accelerated signal processing and embedded system prototyping.
resources.overview: The AEL uses Xilinx Zynq boards that combine a dual-core ARM processor with Xilinx FPGA fabric. These are used for real-time implementation of signal processing algorithms, hardware acceleration of communication baseband functions, and control plane experiments. They interface directly with SDR platforms and the 5G testbed for hardware-in-the-loop experiments.

courses.title: Wireless Systems
courses.description: Research and experimental setups for modern communication systems.

courses.title: Signal Processing
courses.description: Hands-on experiments and prototyping around communication and sensing.

courses.title: Automation
courses.description: Support for robotics, control, and system integration projects.
